paper_title: Geochemical Analysis of HB-503 and Testing the Utility of Geochemical Proxies to Reconstruct Holocene Coastal Environments and Relative Sea Level: A Case Study from Hungry Bay, Bermuda

methods.description: This method involves analyzing sediment cores from site HB-503 to understand the geochemical properties and historical deposition environments. The analysis focuses on δ13C values, C:N ratios, residual carbon, TOC, and Rock-Eval pyrolysis parameters to interpret past environmental conditions and sedimentary processes.

steps.description: Collect sediment cores from site HB-503 for geochemical analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment cores from site HB-503

steps.description: Measure bulk-sediment δ13C values in different sediment layers (freshwater peat, algal gel, mangrove peat).
steps.searchable_summary: measure δ13C values in sediment layers

steps.description: Compare δ13C values with modern analogues to interpret post-depositional changes.
steps.searchable_summary: compare δ13C values with modern analogues

steps.description: Analyze C:N ratios in sediment layers to understand decomposition processes.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze C:N ratios in sediment layers

steps.description: Interpret C:N ratio data to identify sediment sources and decomposition processes.
outputs: interpretation of sediment sources and decomposition

steps.description: Measure residual carbon and TOC in sediment layers.
Perform Rock-Eval pyrolysis to determine S2 hydrocarbons and Tmax values.
Interpret Rock-Eval parameters to assess diagenesis and burial rates.
outputs: assessment of diagenesis and burial rates
